entries.title: High-Flow or Standard Oxygen in Acute Hypoxemic Respiratory Failure.
entries.title_ko: 급성 저산소성 호흡부전에서 고유량 대 표준 산소요법 (SOHO 무작위 임상시험)
entries.journal: The New England journal of medicine
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 급성 저산소성 호흡부전 성인 환자에서 high-flow nasal cannula를 통한 산소 공급이 표준 산소요법에 비해 삽관(intubation)과 28일 사망률을 감소시키는가?

pico.population: [France] Patients who had acute hypoxemic respiratory failure, with a ratio of the partial pressure of arterial oxygen to the fraction of inspired oxygen (PaO2/FiO2) of 200 or less, a respiratory rate of more than 25 breaths per minute, and pulmonary infiltrate on chest imaging; 1116 patients underwent randomization and 1110 (556 high-flow-oxygen, 554 standard-oxygen) were analyzed. Registry inclusion: patients older than 18 years; exclusions included PaCO2 >45 mmHg, need for emergent intubation, hemodynamic instability (vasopressors >0.3 µg/kg/min), and Glasgow Coma Scale ≤12.
pico.intervention: Oxygen delivered through a high-flow nasal cannula (high-flow-oxygen therapy); n=556 analyzed.
pico.outcome: Primary outcome was death by day 28: 14.6% (81 of 556) in the high-flow-oxygen group vs 14.6% (81 of 554) in the standard-oxygen group (difference, -0.05 percentage points; 95% CI, -4.21 to 4.10; P = 0.98).

pico_ko.population: [프랑스] 급성 저산소성 호흡부전 환자로, PaO2/FiO2 비 200 이하, 호흡수 분당 25회 초과, 흉부 영상에서 폐 침윤(pulmonary infiltrate)을 동반한 환자; 1116명이 무작위배정되었고 1110명(high-flow-oxygen 556명, standard-oxygen 554명)이 분석됨. 등록부 기준: 18세 초과 환자; 제외 기준으로 PaCO2 >45 mmHg, 응급 삽관 필요, 혈역학적 불안정(vasopressor >0.3 µg/kg/min), Glasgow Coma Scale ≤12 등이 포함됨.
pico_ko.intervention: High-flow nasal cannula를 통한 산소 공급(high-flow-oxygen 요법); 분석 대상 556명.
pico_ko.outcome: 일차 결과는 28일째 사망: high-flow-oxygen군 14.6%(556명 중 81명) vs standard-oxygen군 14.6%(554명 중 81명) (차이 -0.05 percentage points; 95% CI, -4.21 ~ 4.10; P = 0.98).

keyFindings: 28-day mortality was identical between groups: 14.6% (81/556) with high-flow oxygen vs 14.6% (81/554) with standard oxygen (difference -0.05 percentage points; 95% CI -4.21 to 4.10; P=0.98).
Intubation by day 28 was lower with high-flow oxygen: 42.4% (236/556) vs 48.4% (268/554) (difference -5.93 percentage points; 95% CI -11.78 to -0.08).
Serious adverse events (cardiac arrest or pneumothorax) during spontaneous breathing were numerically more frequent with high-flow oxygen: 13 (2.3%) vs 6 (1.1%).
keyFindings_ko: 28일 사망률은 두 군에서 동일했음: high-flow oxygen 14.6%(81/556) vs standard oxygen 14.6%(81/554) (차이 -0.05 percentage points; 95% CI -4.21 ~ 4.10; P=0.98).
28일째 삽관은 high-flow oxygen군에서 더 낮았음: 42.4%(236/556) vs 48.4%(268/554) (차이 -5.93 percentage points; 95% CI -11.78 ~ -0.08).
자발호흡 중 중대한 이상반응(cardiac arrest 또는 pneumothorax)은 high-flow oxygen군에서 수치상 더 많았음: 13명(2.3%) vs 6명(1.1%).

entries.title: Mortality rates among adults with sepsis and septic shock in Indonesia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
entries.title_ko: 인도네시아 성인 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 환자의 사망률: 체계적 문헌고찰 및 메타분석
entries.journal: Journal of infection in developing countries
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 인도네시아 성인 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 환자에서 통합(pooled) 28일 사망률과 원내(in-hospital) 사망률은 얼마이며, 특히 패혈성 쇼크의 사망 부담은 얼마나 높은가?

pico.population: [Indonesia] Adults with sepsis and septic shock in Indonesia, defined by Sepsis-3, Sepsis-2, and Not-Mentioned criteria; 47 studies were included in this review, identified from PubMed, Scopus, DOAJ, and citation lists.
pico.intervention: Observational assessment of sepsis and septic shock mortality (28-day and in-hospital mortality), with no intervention; this is a systematic review and meta-analysis of mortality rates across Indonesia.
pico.outcome: Pooled 28-day mortality rate of 55.9% (95% CI, 48.7%-63.0%; I2 = 79.5%) among patients with sepsis and septic shock.

pico_ko.population: [인도네시아] Sepsis-3, Sepsis-2, Not-Mentioned 기준으로 정의된 인도네시아 성인 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 환자; PubMed, Scopus, DOAJ 및 인용목록에서 확인된 총 47개 연구가 포함됨.
pico_ko.intervention: 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 사망률(28일 및 in-hospital 사망률)에 대한 관찰적 평가로, 별도의 중재는 없음; 인도네시아 전역의 사망률에 대한 체계적 문헌고찰 및 메타분석임.
pico_ko.outcome: 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 환자에서 통합 28일 사망률 55.9% (95% CI, 48.7%-63.0%; I2 = 79.5%).

keyFindings: Across 47 included studies, the pooled 28-day mortality for adults with sepsis and septic shock in Indonesia was 55.9% (95% CI, 48.7%-63.0%; I2 = 79.5%).
Pooled in-hospital mortality for the combined sepsis/septic shock cohort was higher at 67.4% (95% CI, 62.9%-72.0%; I2 = 95.02%).
Among patients with septic shock specifically, in-hospital mortality reached 77.5% (95% CI, 67.3%-87.8%; I2 = 91.18%).
keyFindings_ko: 포함된 47개 연구를 종합할 때, 인도네시아 성인 패혈증 및 패혈성 쇼크 환자의 통합 28일 사망률은 55.9% (95% CI, 48.7%-63.0%; I2 = 79.5%)였음.
패혈증/패혈성 쇼크 통합 코호트의 통합 in-hospital 사망률은 이보다 높은 67.4% (95% CI, 62.9%-72.0%; I2 = 95.02%)였음.
패혈성 쇼크 환자에 한정하면 in-hospital 사망률은 77.5% (95% CI, 67.3%-87.8%; I2 = 91.18%)에 달했음.

entries.title: Red Blood Cell Transfusion Beyond Restrictive Thresholds in Patients With Septic Shock and an Elevated Lactate Level: A Multicenter Observational Study.
entries.title_ko: 제한적 수혈 역치를 넘어선 적혈구 수혈: 젖산(lactate)이 상승한 패혈성 쇼크 환자에서의 다기관 관찰 연구
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 패혈성 쇼크 환자에서 적혈구(RBC) 수혈(특히 제한적 Hb 역치를 넘어선 수혈)이 원내 사망률을 개선하는가, 그리고 상승된 lactate 수치가 수혈 결정을 안내하는 생리학적 트리거로 쓰일 수 있는가?

pico.population: [South Korea] Adults (age ≥ 19 years) with septic shock presenting to the emergency departments of 16 teaching hospitals, from a prospective multicenter Korean Shock Society (KoSS) registry collected October 2015–December 2022; 8,711 patients included (4 excluded for missing initial Hb), stratified into Hb < 7.0 g/dL (n=535), Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL (n=1,599), and Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL (n=6,577).
pico.intervention: RBC transfusion administered within the first six hours of the ED visit (received by 884/8,711, 10.1%; 510/884 [57.7%] transfused at Hb ≥ 7 g/dL and 140/884 [15.8%] at Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL).
pico.comparison: No RBC transfusion within six hours of the ED visit, within the same Hb stratum.
pico.outcome: In-hospital mortality (multivariable logistic regression, adjusted): no significant association in the Hb < 7.0 g/dL group (aOR 0.86; 95% CI 0.54–1.37) or Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL group (aOR 0.79; 95% CI 0.59–1.06); in the Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL group RBC transfusion was associated with increased in-hospital mortality (aOR 1.99; 95% CI 1.34–2.93). Benefits of transfusion were not confirmed on analysis based on lactate level.

pico_ko.population: [대한민국] 응급실에 내원한 성인(만 19세 이상) 패혈성 쇼크 환자로, 16개 수련병원에서 전향적으로 수집된 다기관 Korean Shock Society(KoSS) 레지스트리(2015년 10월~2022년 12월) 대상; 총 8,711명 포함(초기 Hb 결측 4명 제외), Hb < 7.0 g/dL(n=535), Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL(n=1,599), Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL(n=6,577)로 층화.
pico_ko.intervention: 응급실 내원 후 첫 6시간 이내에 시행한 RBC 수혈(884/8,711명, 10.1%가 시행; 이 중 510명[57.7%]은 Hb ≥ 7 g/dL에서, 140명[15.8%]은 Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL에서 수혈).
pico_ko.comparison: 같은 Hb 층 내에서 응급실 내원 후 6시간 이내 RBC 수혈을 받지 않은 군.
pico_ko.outcome: 원내 사망률(다변량 logistic regression, 보정): Hb < 7.0 g/dL 군(aOR 0.86; 95% CI 0.54–1.37)과 Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL 군(aOR 0.79; 95% CI 0.59–1.06)에서는 유의한 연관 없음; Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL 군에서는 RBC 수혈이 원내 사망률 증가와 연관(aOR 1.99; 95% CI 1.34–2.93). Lactate 수치 기반 분석에서도 수혈의 이득은 확인되지 않음.

keyFindings: Among 8,711 septic shock patients, 10.1% (884) received RBC transfusion within 6 hours, and 57.7% (510/884) of these were transfused at Hb ≥ 7 g/dL — above the restrictive threshold.

RBC transfusion showed no mortality association in the Hb < 7.0 g/dL (aOR 0.86, 95% CI 0.54–1.37) or Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL groups (aOR 0.79, 95% CI 0.59–1.06), but was associated with increased in-hospital mortality in the Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL group (aOR 1.99, 95% CI 1.34–2.93).
Transfused patients in the Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL group had higher initial lactate (median 5.3 vs. 3.5 mmol/L), yet transfusion benefit was not confirmed in lactate-based analysis.
keyFindings_ko: 패혈성 쇼크 환자 8,711명 중 10.1%(884명)가 6시간 이내 RBC 수혈을 받았고, 이 중 57.7%(510/884)는 제한적 역치를 초과한 Hb ≥ 7 g/dL에서 수혈받았다.
RBC 수혈은 Hb < 7.0 g/dL 군(aOR 0.86, 95% CI 0.54–1.37) 및 Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL 군(aOR 0.79, 95% CI 0.59–1.06)에서는 사망률과 연관이 없었으나, Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL 군에서는 원내 사망률 증가와 연관되었다(aOR 1.99, 95% CI 1.34–2.93).
Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL 군의 수혈 환자는 초기 lactate가 더 높았지만(중앙값 5.3 vs. 3.5 mmol/L), lactate 기반 분석에서도 수혈의 이득은 확인되지 않았다.
entries.fullText:
### INTRODUCTION
Patients with septic shock often have anemia, which is associated with a poor outcome. Red blood cells (RBCs) play a critical role in oxygen transport, and RBC transfusion has been used in patients with septic shock to improve tissue oxygenation. Transfusions in critically ill patients are also associated with risks, such as circulatory overload, transmission of infectious diseases, and hypersensitivity reactions. Therefore, a careful balance between the benefits and risks is essential for each patient. Current guidelines recommend a restrictive transfusion strategy in patients with sepsis with a hemoglobin (Hb) threshold of 7 g/dL. However, few studies have examined real-world transfusion practices in patients with septic shock, particularly regarding adherence to these guidelines.
The guidelines recommend that decisions regarding RBC transfusion should not solely rely on the Hb level, but should also consider individual patient-specific factors and overall clinical condition. However, specific recommendations for alternative physiological transfusion triggers have not been provided. This stems from the limited research into alternative transfusion triggers, compared to the extensive evidence supporting Hb transfusion thresholds. In septic shock, an elevated lactate level indicates inadequate perfusion, and recovery from this condition is critical. However, few studies have reported whether RBC transfusion is beneficial for patients with septic shock and an elevated lactate level.
This study examined RBC transfusion practices in patients with septic shock and investigated the differences in in-hospital mortality rates based on the transfusion status across groups with a different Hb level. Additionally, we explored the relationship between transfusion and mortality based on the lactate level in patients with septic shock. We aimed to investigate transfusion practices in patients with septic shock and to examine whether lactate can be used as a physiological marker to trigger transfusion.

### METHODS

### Data source and study cohort
An observational study was conducted using data from a multicenter registry of the Korean Shock Society (KoSS), prospectively collected between October 2015 and December 2022. The KoSS is a collaborative research network dedicated to improving the diagnosis and management of septic shock. The registry includes data collected from patients with septic shock who presented to the emergency departments (EDs) of 16 teaching hospitals in South Korea.
The inclusion criteria for patients in the KoSS registry were: age ≥ 19 years, visit to participating ED with suspected or confirmed infection, and evidence of refractory hypotension or hypoperfusion. Hypotension was defined as systolic blood pressure < 90 mmHg, mean arterial pressure (MAP) < 70 mmHg, or a reduction of > 40 mmHg in systolic blood pressure. Refractory hypotension was defined as persistent hypotension following administration of intravenous fluid (≥ 1L over 30 minutes or 20–30 mL/kg) or the requirement for vasopressors after fluid resuscitation. Hypoperfusion was defined as a serum lactate level ≥ 4 mmol/L. This differs from the Sepsis-3 definition of septic shock, as the KoSS registry began prior to the implementation of the Sepsis-3 definition. Patients who were classified as “Do Not Attempt Resuscitation” at the time of the ED visit, diagnosed more than six hours after the ED visit, or transferred to another hospital during ED treatment were excluded.
Patients with a missing initial Hb value in the registry were excluded. Patients with septic shock were divided into three groups based on their initial Hb level: Hb < 7.0 g/dL, Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL, and Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL. Group classification was based on an Hb level of 7 g/dL for the restrictive transfusion threshold and 9 g/dL for the liberal transfusion threshold.

### Data collection
Demographic and clinical data including age, sex, initial Hb and lactate levels, initial vital signs, medical history, and infection focus were extracted from the KoSS registry. The severity of illness was assessed using the Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score and the Acute Physiology and Chronic Health Evaluation II (APACHE II) score at the time of patient enrollment. The presence of septic shock, as defined by the Sepsis-3 criteria, was also determined. Sepsis-3 shock refers to sepsis with persistent hypotension requiring vasopressors to maintain a MAP ≥ 65 mmHg and a serum lactate level > 2 mmol/L, despite adequate fluid resuscitation.
During the initial period following an ED visit, patients with septic shock often present with unstable vital signs, reflecting the acute phase. During this critical period, fluid resuscitation, administration of antibiotics, and vasopressor therapy are essential interventions that can significantly reduce mortality. The KoSS registry includes variables regarding whether RBC transfusions were administered within six hours and 24 hours of the ED visit. To assess whether RBC transfusion during the acute resuscitation phase was associated with patient outcomes, the independent variable in this study was defined as the administration of RBC transfusion within the first six hours of the ED visit.
To account for the overall quality of septic shock management, we also collected data on adherence to the sepsis resuscitation bundle. These data included the time to antibiotic administration (categorized as < 1 hour, 1–3 hours, or ≥ 3 hours from the ED visit), whether at least 30 mL/kg of crystalloid fluid was administered within 3 hours, the achievement of a MAP > 65 mmHg (typically with vasopressors within 6 hours), and whether source control interventions, such as emergency surgery or percutaneous drainage, were performed.
The primary outcome of this study was in-hospital mortality. First, whether in-hospital mortality differed between the transfusion and non-transfusion groups based on the Hb level was assessed. Subsequently, this outcome was examined within subgroups categorized by the lactate level.

### Statistical analysis
Categorical variables are presented as counts and percentages. Fisher’s exact test was used to compare categorical variables between two groups. The normality of continuous variables was assessed using the Shapiro-Wilk test. Continuous variables with a normal distribution are presented as means and standard deviations, whereas those with a non-normal distribution are presented as medians and interquartile ranges (25th to 75th percentiles). The Student’s -test or the Wilcoxon rank-sum test was applied based on the results of the test for normality.
To evaluate the association between RBC transfusion within six hours and in-hospital mortality, multivariable logistic regression analyses were performed. The model was adjusted for potential confounding factors that might influence in-hospital mortality. These covariates included age, sex, SOFA score, APACHE II score, initial lactate level, and compliance with the sepsis bundle, specifically: timing of antibiotics, adequate fluid administration, use of vasopressors to maintain MAP > 65 mmHg, and source control. The results are presented as adjusted odds ratios (aORs) with 95% confidence intervals (CIs). All statistical analyses were conducted using R software (version 4.4.1; R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria) with the R Studio interface. Statistical significance was determined using two-sided, unpaired tests with a  value < 0.05.

### Ethics statement
This study was approved by the Institutional Review Boards of Hanyang University Hospital (HYUH 2015-11-013-022), and informed consent was obtained before data collection. The study has therefore been performed in accordance with the ethical standards laid down in the 1964 Declaration of Helsinki and its later amendments.

### RESULTS

### Basic characteristics of transfused patients
Of the 8,715 patients registered in the KoSS registry, four with missing Hb data were excluded, resulting in 8,711 being included in the analysis (). Among these, 535 patients had Hb < 7.0 g/dL, 1,599 had Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL, and 6,577 had Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL. The percentage of patients who received an RBC transfusion was 10.1% (884/8,711). For patients with Hb < 7.0 g/dL, Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL, and Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL, 69.9% (374/535), 23.1% (370/1,599), and 2.1% (140/6,577), respectively, received an RBC transfusion. Among the 884 patients who received an RBC transfusion, 510 (57.7%) were transfused at an Hb level ≥ 7 g/dL, exceeding the restrictive threshold (). Furthermore, 15.8% (140/884) of the patients who received an RBC transfusion were transfused at an Hb level ≥ 9.0 g/dL, which exceeds the liberal transfusion threshold.
To identify the factors associated with RBC transfusion, the baseline characteristics were compared between the transfusion and non-transfusion groups (). For the patients in the Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL and Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL groups, the transfusion group was younger than the non-transfusion group, whereas the Hb < 7.0 g/dL group had a higher percentage of females in the transfusion group. The severity of illness, as indicated by the SOFA score and the APACHE II score, was higher in the transfusion group than in the non-transfusion group across all groups of Hb level. In all groups, the initial Hb level was lower in the transfusion group than in the non-transfusion group. In terms of comorbidities, there was a greater percentage of patients with hematological malignancies in the transfusion group than in the non-transfusion group. For patients with metastatic cancer, this tendency was only in the Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL group.
In the Hb 7.0–8.9 g/dL and Hb ≥ 9.0 g/dL groups, the transfusion group had a higher heart rate and a greater percentage of patients met the Sepsis-3 criteria for shock compared to the non-transfusion group. entries.title: Acceptability and Fidelity of a Cognitive Rehabilitation Intervention During and After Intensive Care: A Feasibility Evaluation.
entries.title_ko: 중환자 치료 중 및 이후 인지 재활 중재의 수용성과 충실도: 실행가능성 평가
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: ICU CogHab 인지 재활 중재(Mindfulness 및 Brain Training)가 ICU에서 퇴원 후 회복 경로 전반에 걸쳐 ICU 환자와 간호사에게 수용 가능하며 충실도 있게 전달되는가?

pico.population: [Denmark] ICU patients and nurses in four medical and surgical ICUs across two Danish university hospitals (~30 ICU beds); patients eligible if expected to require ICU admission for more than 24 h, able to communicate in Danish, and awake and without delirium at inclusion. Of 115 patients meeting eligibility criteria, 83 were enrolled in the parent feasibility trial; 31 patients contributed survey data and 9 participated in interviews, and 53 nurses contributed 194 session-based surveys and 7 interviews.
pico.intervention: The ICU CogHab intervention comprising two structured cognitive rehabilitation components — Mindfulness (brief audio-guided exercises delivered via QR codes supporting attentional and emotional regulation) and Brain Training (graded cognitive exercises targeting attention, memory and executive function via a structured activity book), delivered from ICU admission to 6-month follow-up, with two arms additionally receiving structured post-ICU support (in-person visits, telephone calls, or text messages).
pico.comparison: Usual care (structured cognitive rehabilitation not routinely integrated), within a five-arm randomised controlled feasibility trial; usual-care patients were not interviewed.
pico.outcome: Acceptability (affective attitude, burden, perceived effectiveness per the Theoretical Framework of Acceptability) and fidelity (adherence and dose per Carroll et al.): both components were generally perceived as relevant and appropriate by patients and nurses, and demonstrated overall acceptability across ICU and post-discharge phases, while fidelity varied across settings and over time. Quantitative data were analysed descriptively; no comparative effect estimates or p-values were reported.

pico_ko.population: [덴마크] 두 개 덴마크 대학병원의 내과·외과 ICU 4곳(총 약 30 ICU 병상)의 ICU 환자와 간호사; 환자는 24시간 초과 ICU 입원이 예상되고 덴마크어로 의사소통이 가능하며 등록 시 각성 상태이고 섬망이 없는 경우 적격. 적격 기준을 충족한 115명 중 83명이 모(母) 실행가능성 시험에 등록되었고, 환자 31명이 설문 데이터를, 9명이 인터뷰에 참여했으며, 간호사 53명이 194건의 세션 기반 설문과 7건의 인터뷰에 기여함.
pico_ko.intervention: 두 가지 구조화된 인지 재활 요소로 구성된 ICU CogHab 중재 — Mindfulness(QR 코드로 제공되는 짧은 오디오 가이드 운동으로 주의력 및 정서 조절 지원)와 Brain Training(구조화된 활동북을 통해 주의력·기억·집행기능을 목표로 하는 단계별 인지 운동) — 을 ICU 입원부터 6개월 추적까지 제공하며, 두 개 군은 추가로 구조화된 퇴원 후 지원(대면 방문, 전화, 문자 메시지)을 받음.
pico_ko.comparison: 5-arm 무작위 대조 실행가능성 시험 내에서의 usual care(구조화된 인지 재활이 일상 진료에 통합되지 않음); usual care 환자는 인터뷰하지 않음.
pico_ko.outcome: 수용성(Theoretical Framework of Acceptability에 따른 affective attitude, burden, perceived effectiveness)과 충실도(Carroll et al.에 따른 adherence 및 dose): 두 요소 모두 환자와 간호사에게 대체로 관련성 있고 적절하다고 인식되었으며 ICU 및 퇴원 후 단계 전반에서 전반적 수용성을 보였으나, 충실도는 환경과 시간에 따라 변동함. 정량 데이터는 기술적으로 분석되었고 비교 효과 추정치나 p-value는 보고되지 않음.

keyFindings: Both intervention components (Mindfulness and Brain Training) were generally perceived as relevant, appropriate, meaningful and supportive by patients and nurses, demonstrating overall acceptability across ICU and post-discharge phases; 31 patients contributed survey data, 9 were interviewed, and 53 nurses contributed 194 session-based surveys plus 7 interviews.

Fidelity (adherence and dose) varied across settings and over time, with intervention use requiring adaptation in timing and duration across recovery phases, influenced by clinical condition, fatigue, patient readiness and competing rehabilitation demands.

Nurses reported contextual barriers to delivery including high workload and competing clinical priorities, indicating that fixed standardized delivery is unlikely and flexible, integrated delivery is needed before larger-scale evaluation.
keyFindings_ko: 두 중재 요소(Mindfulness 및 Brain Training)는 환자와 간호사에게 대체로 관련성 있고 적절하며 의미 있고 지지적인 것으로 인식되어 ICU 및 퇴원 후 단계 전반에서 전반적 수용성을 보였음; 환자 31명이 설문에, 9명이 인터뷰에 참여했고, 간호사 53명이 194건의 세션 기반 설문과 7건의 인터뷰에 기여함.
충실도(adherence 및 dose)는 환경과 시간에 따라 변동했으며, 임상 상태·피로·환자 준비도·경쟁적 재활 요구에 영향을 받아 회복 단계 전반에서 타이밍과 지속시간의 조정이 필요했음.
간호사는 높은 업무량과 경쟁적 임상 우선순위를 포함한 전달 장벽을 보고했으며, 이는 고정된 표준화 전달이 어렵고 대규모 평가 전에 유연하고 통합된 전달이 필요함을 시사함.
entries.fullText:
### Introduction
Post‐Intensive Care Syndrome (PICS) has been increasingly recognised over the past two decades and is defined as  []. Among these domains, cognitive impairment is one of the most persistent and under‐recognised sequelae of critical illness [, ]. Many ICU survivors experience deficits in attention, memory and executive functioning months or years after discharge [, ], which may substantially affect everyday life, social participation, return to work and long‐term healthcare utilisation [, ].

### Background
Research highlights the lived consequences of post‐ICU cognitive impairment, with survivors describing memory problems, challenges in managing daily activities and the feeling of insufficient support during recovery [, ]. At the same time, nurses in intensive care report uncertainty and a lack of structured, evidence‐based approaches to address cognitive rehabilitation in the early phases of critical illness []. Together, these findings indicate a gap in care across the transition from ICU admission to the ward and into post‐discharge recovery.
Emerging evidence from cognitive rehabilitation research within critical illness, including the RETURN I and II trials, suggests that structured cognitive interventions may support recovery when delivered early and consistently [, ]. However, evidence remains limited regarding how such interventions are perceived, delivered and sustained across different phases of recovery.
Drawing on principles from neurorehabilitation and mindfulness‐based approaches, strategies targeting structured cognitive stimulation, attentional control and emotional regulation may be particularly relevant for ICU survivors with varying needs and fluctuating capacity [, , ].
The ICU CogHab intervention was developed to address this gap, and it comprises two intervention components, Mindfulness and Brain Training, delivered from ICU admission to six months after ICU discharge (Astrup et al. under review). Within the feasibility study, patients were randomised to Mindfulness, Brain Training or usual care. Evaluation of how these components are perceived, delivered and used in practice is therefore needed.

### Aim
The aim of this study was to evaluate the feasibility of the ICU CogHab intervention, with particular focus on acceptability and fidelity among ICU patients and nurses, to inform further intervention refinement and future evaluation.

### Methods

### Study Design
This feasibility study reports the acceptability and fidelity of a cognitive rehabilitation intervention (ICU CogHab), evaluated within a five‐arm randomised controlled feasibility trial with 6‐month follow‐up. The trial comprised four intervention groups (two with post‐ICU support and two without) and a usual care group. Evaluation of study design feasibility outcomes, including recruitment, retention and trial procedures, has been reported separately [].
Guided by the Medical Research Council (MRC) framework for complex interventions [], the present multi‐method feasibility evaluation combined quantitative survey data with qualitative interviews to explore experiences of the intervention components in terms of relevance, perceived burden and effect and contextual influences on fidelity (i.e., delivery and engagement).
A 12‐month inclusion period was selected to generate sufficient quantitative and qualitative data for feasibility evaluation while accounting for expected attrition. Quantitative survey components are reported in accordance with the Consensus‐Based Checklist for Reporting of Survey Studies (CROSS) [], and qualitative interviews followed the Consolidated Criteria for Reporting Qualitative Research (COREQ) [].

### Intervention
The ICU CogHab intervention comprised two structured cognitive rehabilitation components: Mindfulness and Brain Training. Patients were allocated to either: (1) Mindfulness with ICU support only, (2) Brain Training with ICU support only, (3) Mindfulness with ICU and post‐ICU support, (4) Brain Training with ICU and post‐ICU support or (5) usual care. Both components were delivered using dedicated intervention boxes containing all relevant materials and were intended to accompany patients from ICU admission until 6 months post‐discharge. ICU nurses (hereafter referred to as nurses) were trained to initiate and support intervention use during admission as part of routine care. Patients received written instructions, and relatives were also encouraged to assist and support engagement when feasible. The Mindfulness component consisted of brief, audio‐guided exercises delivered via QR codes to support attentional and emotional regulation. The Brain Training component included graded cognitive exercises targeting attention, memory and executive function, delivered through a structured activity book and simple supporting tools. The intervention was designed to be flexible, allowing adaptation according to individual capacity and preferences.
Two intervention groups additionally received structured post‐ICU support for up to 6 months following discharge, consisting of planned follow‐up contacts (e.g., in‐person visits, telephone calls, or text messages) to encourage continued engagement. Detailed descriptions of intervention development, content and delivery are reported elsewhere (Astrup et al. under review) and in accordance with the template for intervention description and replication (TIDieR) checklist [].

### Setting and Participants
The study was conducted in four medical and surgical ICUs across two Danish university hospitals, including approximately 30 ICU beds in total, providing high‐dependency care, including invasive mechanical ventilation and continuous haemodynamic monitoring, with predominantly 1:1 nurse‐to‐patient staffing. Daily patient care was guided by principles aligned with national and international recommendations [, ], including light or minimal sedation enabling many patients to remain awake and participate in care, routine delirium assessment, early mobilisation and supportive strategies such as sleep promotion, reorientation, communication support and active involvement of relatives. These practices were considered important contextual conditions underpinning the intervention, as they were expected to support attentional engagement and readiness to participate in cognitive rehabilitation activities during ICU admission. However, structured cognitive rehabilitation was not routinely integrated into usual care.
In the parent feasibility trial, patients were eligible if they were expected to require ICU admission for more than 24 h, were able to communicate in Danish and were awake and without evidence of delirium at inclusion according to routine clinical assessment. Across the participating ICUs, 115 patients met the eligibility criteria, of whom 83 were enrolled in the feasibility trial [].
All consenting patients randomised to an intervention group were asked to complete intervention evaluation surveys following ICU discharge and through 6‐month follow‐up. Patients eligible for qualitative interviews were those who had used the intervention partially or until follow‐up and had consented to interview participation. Patients allocated to usual care were not interviewed.
Across the four ICUs, nurses delivered the intervention components as part of routine care. When caring for patients allocated to an intervention group, they were instructed, where feasible, to support use of the materials and complete brief surveys related to the intervention delivery and use. Nurses with direct delivery experience were subsequently invited to participate in qualitative interviews.

### Feasibility Outcomes
The feasibility evaluation focused on two primary domains: acceptability and fidelity. Acceptability was informed by three domains from the Theoretical Framework of Acceptability (TFA) (affective attitude, burden, perceived effectiveness) [], and fidelity was guided by Carroll et al. [] and operationalised through measures of adherence and dose. Indicators related to quality of delivery and participant responsiveness were examined within the adherence domain, with patient‐reported data reflecting intervention use and engagement, and nurse‐reported data reflecting delivery and practical implementation. Quantitative survey data and qualitative interviews were used to assess predefined domains within these frameworks. An overview of domains, definitions and data sources is provided in Table .
[Table: TABLE 1 — Data collection in relation to the acceptability and fidelity domains, including definitions and measurement methods.]

### Data Collection

### Quantitative Data Collection
Quantitative data were collected using two surveys: an ICU‐based survey completed by the nurses after each intervention session during ICU admission, and a follow‐up survey completed weekly by patients after ICU discharge until 6‐month follow‐up (distributed every Sunday for 26 weeks).
Survey instruments were developed specifically for this study and underwent face validation by the research group and experienced ICU nurses ( = 3) to ensure clarity and alignment with predefined feasibility domains. Items included multiple‐choice questions and 5‐point Likert scale ratings (1 = strongly disagree to 5 = strongly agree) []. Survey items were designed to capture key indicators of acceptability (e.g., perceived relevance and appropriateness in relation to patients' energy level) and fidelity (e.g., use, frequency, duration, content chosen and level of support). Given the clinical condition of ICU patients and the longitudinal follow‐up design, survey length was intentionally kept brief to minimise burden and support completion. Both surveys also included optional free‐text fields allowing patients and nurses to provide additional comments on intervention use and experiences. During ICU admission, the ICU survey captured both nurse‐reported information on intervention delivery and patient‐reported

entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 성인 중환자실 환자의 가족 및 돌봄제공자에서, 비약물적 중재(교육적·심리적·정보제공적·실용적 중재)가 불안, 우울, PTSD, 삶의 질 저하 같은 post-intensive care syndrome-family(PICS-F) 증상을 치료하는 데 효과적인가?

pico.population: [International] Family members and/or caregivers with PICS-F of adult critically ill patients; seven RCTs published between 2018 and 2025 involving 1026 participants (439 in the intervention group and 587 in the control/comparison group), predominantly women. Family members of pediatric patients and studies of PICS in patients themselves were excluded.
pico.intervention: Non-pharmacological interventions — educational, psychological, informative, or practical therapies/interventions aimed at improving PICS-F outcomes (e.g., counseling, education, support programs, structured longitudinal follow-up, personalized emotional support, active professional involvement, multicomponent approaches).
pico.comparison: Control or comparison group (usual care/standard care) as randomized in the included RCTs (587 participants).
pico.outcome: PICS-F symptoms (anxiety and depression measured by HADS; PTSD measured by IES-R/IES-6) and quality of life. Most interventions did not demonstrate statistically significant improvements; however, 2 multicomponent interventions combining structured follow-up, personalized support, and active professional involvement showed significant reductions in anxiety, depression, and PTSD symptoms and improvements in quality of life. Meta-analyses were random-effects (RevMan 5.4) with heterogeneity assessed by the I² statistic and significance set at p < .05; no pooled effect estimates were reported in the provided text.

pico_ko.outcome: PICS-F 증상(HADS로 측정한 불안 및 우울; IES-R/IES-6으로 측정한 PTSD)과 삶의 질. 대부분의 중재는 통계적으로 유의한 개선을 보이지 못했으나, 구조화된 추적관찰·개별 맞춤 지지·적극적 전문가 개입을 결합한 2개의 다요소 중재는 불안·우울·PTSD 증상의 유의한 감소와 삶의 질 개선을 보임. 메타분석은 random-effects 모델(RevMan 5.4)로 수행하고 이질성은 I² 통계량으로 평가했으며 유의수준은 p < .05로 설정, 제공된 본문에는 통합 효과추정치(pooled effect estimate)가 보고되지 않음.

keyFindings: Seven RCTs (2018–2025) with 1026 participants (439 intervention, 587 control), predominantly women, evaluated non-pharmacological interventions for PICS-F.
Most interventions did not demonstrate statistically significant improvements in PICS-F symptoms (anxiety, depression, PTSD, quality of life).

Only 2 multicomponent interventions combining structured follow-up, personalized support, and active professional involvement showed significant reductions in anxiety, depression, and PTSD and improved quality of life, whereas brief/standardized/self-guided interventions were generally ineffective.
keyFindings_ko: 7개 RCT(2018~2025), 1026명(중재군 439명, 대조군 587명, 대다수 여성)에서 PICS-F에 대한 비약물적 중재를 평가.
대부분의 중재는 PICS-F 증상(불안·우울·PTSD·삶의 질)에서 통계적으로 유의한 개선을 보이지 못함.
구조화된 추적관찰·개별 맞춤 지지·적극적 전문가 개입을 결합한 2개의 다요소 중재만이 불안·우울·PTSD의 유의한 감소와 삶의 질 개선을 보였고, 짧거나 표준화되거나 자가안내식 중재는 대체로 효과가 없었음.
entries.fullText:
### 1. Introduction
The intensive care unit (ICU) is a highly specialized healthcare environment designed to provide advanced life support and continuous monitoring to critically ill patients, with the aim of improving outcomes in complex clinical situations. However, the ICU also generates a significant negative impact not only on patients but also on their family members or primary caregivers.
Among these adverse effects, special attention has been given to the so-called post-intensive care syndrome, defined as a set of physical, psychological, and cognitive impairments affecting patients who have been admitted to an ICU. Furthermore, the consequences of ICU admission can also affect patients’ relatives or primary caregivers.
Post-intensive care syndrome-family (PICS-F) refers to a condition affecting the primary caregiver (whether a family member or not) of a patient who has been admitted to an ICU, manifesting as physical, psychological, or social disturbances. Its prevalence ranges from 20% to 40%.
The main risk factors for developing this syndrome include personal variables (low educational level, taking a leading role in clinical decision-making during the ICU stay, age, female gender, unemployment, or having recently lost a loved one or witnessed a severe health crisis), healthcare-related factors (poor communication with healthcare staff, lack of information, and restricted visitation), and patient-related factors (severity of illness, delirium, mechanical ventilation, and among others).
The main physical manifestations of PICS-F are fatigue, sleep disturbances, and general decline. Psychological consequences include emotional distress, anxiety, depression, and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). In some cases, anxiety and depression may worsen, leading to hospitalization. On a social level, financial insecurity, deterioration of social relationships, and caregiver burden are observed. These physical, psychological, and social consequences of PICS-F not only affect family members on an individual level but also lead to increased healthcare costs, greater use of health services, and more frequent work absenteeism, thus having a significant impact on healthcare systems and the global economy.
Several recommendations exist to prevent PICS-F. The most relevant include reducing the burden on family members or primary caregivers – namely, allowing flexible presence with the patient and adjusting their caregiving role, which contributes to lower rates of depression and anxiety; fostering effective communication between family members and healthcare staff, including accessible and up-to-date information about the patient’s clinical status. It is also beneficial to involve nurses and/or social workers trained to manage these situations. Moreover, allowing family members to participate in patient care during the ICU stay can help them feel useful and reduce anxiety levels.
Currently, there are no universally validated questionnaires or scales for the evaluation or diagnosis of PICS-F. However, commonly used tools for assessing psychological symptoms include the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) for anxiety and depression, the Impact of Event Scale-Revised (IES-R/IES‑6) for PTSD, and the Zarit Burden Interview for caregiver burden.
Regarding interventions for its treatment, key strategies include providing accurate and timely information and communication about the patient’s condition in the ICU, the use of diaries to record ICU events for later review, follow-up after discharge, and bereavement support in the case of a patient’s death.
Nevertheless, there is currently no consensus on the effectiveness of these interventions or the specific conditions under which they should be applied. For this reason, it is necessary to critically assess the efficacy of available interventions.
To date, no updated systematic reviews have analyzed the impact of non-pharmacological therapies or interventions in treating PICS-F. Therefore, the objective of this systematic review was to identify and analyze different non-pharmacological interventions aimed at treating PICS-F in relatives and primary caregivers of adult patients and to evaluate their effectiveness in improving physical, psychological, and social outcomes.
The hypothesis proposed was that applying interventions for PICS-F would improve its associated symptoms.

### 2. Methods

### 2.1. Design and sources of information
A systematic review was conducted following the guidelines of the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) statement.
This systematic review was registered in International Prospective Register of Ongoing Systematic Reviews under registration number CRD420251075110.

### 2.2. Research question
The research was conducted using the Population, Intervention, Comparison, and Outcomes (PICO) framework, as shown in Table .
The clinical question was as follows: Are educational, psychological, informative, or practical interventions or therapies effective in treating PICS-F?
To answer this, a search was conducted in the following databases: PubMed, Web of Science, Scopus, and ClinicalTrials.gov between April 1 and July 31, 2025. The search strategy is detailed in Table .
[Table: Table 1 — Research question in PICO format.]
[Table: Table 2 — Search strategy used for each database.]

### 2.3. Inclusion and exclusion criteria
The inclusion criteria for this systematic review were as follows: randomized controlled trials (RCTs) with a control group (CG); family members and/or caregivers with PICS-F; and studies analyzing educational, psychological, informative, or practical interventions or therapies aimed at improving the analyzed variables.
The exclusion criteria were as follows: family members of pediatric patients diagnosed with PICS-F; studies evaluating the efficacy of treatment for PICS in patients; and studies without measurable outcomes.
This systematic review included only RCTs that evaluated various therapies or interventions to address PICS-F. This selection was made to ensure the highest level of scientific evidence and methodological rigor.

### 2.4. Study selection process
The study selection process was conducted collaboratively by LPPM and NME, following the established inclusion and exclusion criteria.
Citations were organized and duplicates removed using the Mendeley reference manager. After duplicate removal, titles and abstracts were reviewed to identify studies for full-text evaluation.
Following a full-text review, the final selection of studies to be included in the qualitative and quantitative synthesis was made. In cases where discrepancies arose during the selection process, discussions were held between LPPM and NME and, if consensus was not reached, a third author, JALA, was consulted to resolve disagreements and reach a consensus.

### 2.5. Quality assessment of the studies
To assess the quality of the selected studies and detect bias, the Cochrane RoB-2 tool for RCTs was used. The scores for each study are presented in Figure .
The RoB-2 tool consists of 5 domains that evaluate: risk of bias in the randomization process; deviations from intended interventions; missing outcome data; measurement of the outcome variables; and selection of the reported results. Each domain contains 22 items with possible responses of Yes, Probably Yes, Probably No, No, Not Applicable (N/A), and Not Reported (N/R).
For each domain, risk of bias was calculated and categorized as low, high, or concerning. The overall risk of bias was determined as follows: if all domains had low risk, the overall risk was categorized as low; if any domain had a concerning risk, the overall risk was categorized as concerning; and if any domain had a high risk or if several domains were concerning, the overall risk was categorized as high.
[Figure: Figure 1. — Application of the RoB-2 tool for randomized clinical trials. NA = not available.]

### 2.6. Data extraction
Data extraction was carried out by LPPM and JMCT. The results were extracted using Mendeley, manually classified, and thoroughly reviewed. The following information was extracted from each article: first author, year, and country; study design; participant characteristics, including sample size, mean age, gender, and selection process; study intervention; variables studied (including the measurement tools used); and main outcomes, outcome assessment, and limitations.

### 2.7. Data analysis
A narrative synthesis was performed for each study included in this review. The influence of interventions on PICS-F was analyzed before and after the intervention.
For the quantitative synthesis, random-effects meta-analyses were performed using RevMan 5.4 (The Cochrane Collaboration). The analyses compared the scores obtained from the different instruments used to assess PICS-F symptoms in order to determine the effectiveness of the interventions. Effect sizes were calculated based on post-intervention outcomes reported by the included studies. Statistical heterogeneity was assessed using the  statistic and was interpreted as follows:  ≤ 25% indicated low heterogeneity, 26% to 50% indicated moderate heterogeneity, and ≥51% indicated high heterogeneity. Statistical significance was set at  < .05.

### 3. Results

### 3.1. Study selection and characteristics
Four databases were consulted, and after applying the previously defined search strategy, a total of 832 articles were retrieved. Of these, 397 were duplicates, resulting in 435 unique articles. After screening titles and abstracts, 12 full-text articles were assessed, and only 7 RCTs published between 2018 and 2025 were selected for analysis.
In total, the included studies involved 1026 participants, of whom 439 were assigned to the intervention group (IG) and 587 to the control or comparison group. Figure  shows the PRISMA flow diagram developed for the selection process in this systematic review.
All studies included ad

entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 지역사회 발병 sepsis로 수액 소생술 적응증이 있는 성인에서, 병원 도착 6시간 이내 30 mL/kg 이상의 수액 투여가 주요 대상군(중증 심장·신장 동반질환 환자, intermediate lactate 상승만 있는 환자 포함)에 걸쳐 30일 사망률 감소와 연관되는가?

pico.population: [USA] Adults hospitalized for community-onset sepsis in 67 hospitals in the Michigan Hospital Medicine Safety Consortium (discharge dates from December 2021 to January 2025) who had an indication for fluid resuscitation (ie, hypotension or lactate level of 18.0 mg/dL or greater) within 3 hours of hospital arrival. Among 43 321 patients hospitalized for community-onset sepsis, 25 481 (58.8%) had an indication for fluid resuscitation and were included (median age, 71 years [IQR, 61-80 years]; 50.5% male; 37.0% with body mass index >30.0). Target populations: 12 943 (50.8%) hypoperfusion without severe comorbidities; 1741 (6.8%) hypoperfusion with severe comorbidities; 9974 (39.1%) intermediate lactate elevation without severe comorbidities; and 823 (3.2%) intermediate lactate elevation with severe comorbidities.
pico.intervention: Receipt of at least 30 mL/kg of fluid in the first 6 hours after hospital arrival. Fluid volume included all crystalloid fluid and blood products.
pico.comparison: Receipt of less than 30 mL/kg of fluid in the first 6 hours after hospital arrival.
pico.outcome: 30-day mortality (adjusted, using IPTW-weighted regression). For ≥30 mL/kg vs <30 mL/kg: hypoperfusion without severe comorbidities 26.0% (95% CI, 24.9%-27.2%) vs 30.4% (95% CI, 28.8%-32.0%), adjusted absolute difference −4.4 pp (95% CI, −6.1 to −2.7 pp); intermediate lactate elevation without severe comorbidities 12.0% (95% CI, 10.6%-13.5%) vs 13.9% (95% CI, 12.9%-14.8%), diff −1.8 pp (95% CI, −3.6 to −0.1 pp); hypoperfusion with severe comorbidities 34.7% (95% CI, 30.8%-38.6%) vs 38.8% (95% CI, 35.8%-41.8%), diff −4.1 pp (95% CI, −9.0 to 0.8 pp; not statistically significant).

entries.fullText:
### Introduction
Intravenous fluids are a key component of sepsis management. The Surviving Sepsis Campaign (SSC) guidelines suggest administering crystalloid fluid (≥30 mL/kg) to patients with sepsis-induced hypoperfusion within 3 hours of sepsis onset. However, there is uncertainty regarding the benefits of fluid resuscitation in patients with severe comorbidities or intermediate lactate elevation (lactate level 18-36 mg/dL; to convert to mmol/L, multiply by 0.111). While SSC guidelines suggest at least 30 mL/kg of fluid resuscitation regardless of comorbidities, clinicians are often reluctant to administer fluid to patients with cardiac or kidney disease due to concerns about risk of fluid overload. Additionally, prospective observational studies suggest that patients with intermediate lactate elevation may benefit from fluid resuscitation, yet such patients are rarely targeted in sepsis initiatives.
Beyond questions about the optimal target population for fluid resuscitation in sepsis, prior guidelines have not specified how to operationalize weight-based fluid dosing. For patients at extremes of weight, dosing can vary markedly when using actual vs ideal or adjusted body weight to calculate fluid volumes, and the optimal approach to weight-based fluid dosing is unclear.
To inform guidance on fluid resuscitation for a statewide quality improvement initiative in Michigan as well as for practice more broadly, we evaluated the association between administration of 30 mL/kg or more of fluid within 6 hours of hospital arrival and 30-day mortality in patients with community-onset sepsis. We evaluated this association across clinically important target populations and using 3 common approaches for weight-based fluid dosing.

### Methods
This was a cohort study of adult patients in 67 hospitals in the Michigan Hospital Medicine Safety Consortium (HMS) Sepsis Registry (discharge dates were from December 2021 to January 2025). HMS is a multihospital collaborative quality initiative funded by Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan and Blue Care Network. The HMS Sepsis Registry includes data on a random sample of patients hospitalized for community-onset sepsis at participating Michigan hospitals. Sepsis hospitalizations are identified through a 2-step process to optimize sensitivity and specificity while avoiding biases introduced by variable sepsis coding practices. Using this approach, we identified a cohort of patients with confirmed community-onset sepsis. Data were collected by professional abstractors at each site using standardized abstraction tools. Outcomes to 30 days were captured for all patients. eAppendixes 1 and 2 in  provide details. This study was deemed not regulated by the University of Michigan institutional review board because HMS is a quality improvement initiative. We followed the Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology () reporting guideline.

### Target Populations for Fluid Resuscitation
In our primary analysis, we considered 4 target populations () based on (1) indication for fluid resuscitation: hypoperfusion (hypotension or lactate level >36.0 mg/dL) vs intermediate lactate elevation (18.0-36.0 mg/dL) and (2) the presence vs absence of a severe comorbidity that might increase the risk of fluid overload: left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) less than 40%, severe-to-critical aortic stenosis, and/or end-stage kidney disease (ESKD). We used data from the first 3 hours after hospital arrival to assign patients to a target population. Our definition of hypoperfusion aligns with common triggers for fluid resuscitation in sepsis: hypotension or lactate level greater than 36.0 mg/dL. Hypotension was defined as mean arterial pressure less than 65 mm Hg, systolic blood pressure less than 90 mm Hg, or vasopressor initiation. Presence of LVEF less than 40% or severe-to-critical aortic stenosis prior to the index admission was determined via review of clinical documentation and echocardiography results. Presence of ESKD prior to admission was defined as documentation of history of chronic dialysis or stage V chronic kidney disease (CKD) not receiving dialysis.
Severe cardiac and kidney comorbidities were combined because both potentially increase the risk of fluid overload and are often considered together in decisions about fluid resuscitation. Given the small number of patients with these severe comorbidities, the power to evaluate each individually was limited. In secondary analyses, we additionally evaluated patients with baseline history of (1) congestive heart failure (CHF), defined as any documented history of heart failure or cardiomyopathy regardless of LVEF, or (2) moderate-to-severe CKD.

### Weight-Based Fluid Volume
While guidelines suggest weight-based fluid dosing, there are multiple approaches to operationalizing the approach to weight-based fluid dosing in practice. Therefore, we considered 3 approaches used by HMS hospitals (), which we term “tailored,” “SEP-1,” and “pragmatic.” The tailored approach uses actual body weight, ideal body weight (IBW), and adjusted body weight depending on a patient’s body mass index (BMI, calculated as weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared), akin to weight-based medication dosing (IBW for males is calculated as 50 + [0.91 × (height in centimeters − 152.4)] and for females, 45.5 + (0.91 × [height in centimeters − 152.4]; adjusted body weight is calculated as 0.4 × [actual body weight − IBW] + IBW). The SEP-1 approach—based on the US Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Severe Sepsis and Septic Shock Management Bundle (SEP-1)—doses fluids based on actual body weight if BMI is 30 or less and on IBW if BMI is more than 30. The pragmatic approach caps fluid volume for patients with BMI greater than 30 so they receive the same fluid volume as a patient with BMI of 30 (capped volume = [height in m] × 30 × 30 mL/kg).
[Figure: Figure 1.  — A, Tailored approach similar to that used by pharmacists for kidney function equations and weight-based medication dosing (weight less than ideal body weight [IBW], use actual weight; weight from IBW to body mass index [BMI] of ≤30 [calculated as weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared], use ideal weight; BMI >30, use adjusted weight). B, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Severe Sepsis and Septic Shock Management Bundle approach (use actual weight for patients with BMI ≤30 and IBW for patients with BMI >30). C, A more pragmatic approach capping fluid volume for patients with BMI more than 30, who receive the amount of fluid a patient with a BMI of 30 would receive based on height and sex.]

### Exposure
The primary exposure was receipt of at least 30 mL/kg of fluid within 6 hours of hospital arrival. Data from the first 3 hours were used to determine eligibility for fluid resuscitation and target population assignment; thus, all patients had at least 3 hours to receive fluid resuscitation after meeting enrollment criteria, consistent with SSC guidance to deliver 30 mL/kg or more within 3 hours. Fluid volumes included all crystalloid fluids and blood products given within 6 hours of hospital arrival. Our approach to calculating fluid volume is described in eAppendix 3 in .

### Outcome
The primary outcome was 30-day mortality. Mortality was determined for all patients in the HMS Sepsis Registry via medical record review, public obituary data, and follow-up by telephone, email, and/or short message service messaging.

### Handling of Missing Data
Missing heights and weights were imputed in primary analyses. If only height or weight was missing, we imputed the other assuming BMI = 30. If both height and weight were missing, we imputed both using Centers for Disease Control and Prevention population averages by sex. As described in eAppendix 3 in , fluid volumes were determined from available documentation using a hierarchical approach and were assumed to be 0 only if no data were available to calculate or infer the volume administered. Missing data for other covariates (eg, laboratory analyses, vital signs) were imputed as normal, following conventional risk-adjustment practice.

### Statistical Analysis
Full details of the study protocol are presented in eTable 1 in . To control for confounding, we used both weighting and regression adjustment in all analyses. First, we used inverse probability of treatment weighting (IPTW) to balance patients on probability of receiving 30 mL/kg or more of fluid. The propensity score model included prespecified patient characteristics (listed in eTable 2 in ) and a random effect for hospital (except in 1 population—patients with intermediate lactate elevation and severe comorbidities—in which the model would not converge). We assessed balance of patient characteristics to ensure good balance (defined as standardized mean difference [SMD] <0.10). Then, we fit a series of hierarchical IPTW-weighted logistic regression models assessing the association between fluid volume and 30-day mortality in the weighted populations, adjusting for the same covariates as the propensity model to further control for confounding and yield precise estimates of treatment effect. Hospital was included as a random effect in both the IPTW and IPTW-weighted logistic regression models. We estimated probabilities (predictive margins) for each exposure level by averaging estimated outcomes over the covariate distribution of the weighted population. Absolute risk differences were estimated as the difference between predictive margins, with 95% CIs derived using the delta method. This approach was applied across all sensitivity analyses. Results were considered statistically significant if the 95% CIs did not cross 0.
We completed multiple sensitivity analyses. First, we examined the association between mortality and fluid volume, modeled as (1) continuous exposure, using restricted cubic spline terms added to adjusted logistic regression model

pico.population: [Country NR] A simulated (in silico) microcirculatory network of one million parallel arterioles in Python (JupyterLab v4.3.4), with vessel radii drawn from a truncated normal distribution (physiological bounds 0–100 microns), mean radius varied 20–30 microns and standard deviation 1.5–3 microns; not human or animal subjects but a computational model of the human microcirculation (true parallel arteriole number probably closer to 100 million, scaled down by a factor of 100 for computation).
pico.intervention: A conceptual and computational model in which each vessel's flow demand (Q) is set by an 'apparent shear target' derived from the laminar shear equation (dynamic viscosity 0.003 Pa·s; apparent shear target 10–20 Pa), with total required cardiac output computed as the sum of individual vessel demands; simulated global vasodilation via rising mean vessel radius (20→30 microns).
pico.comparison: Baseline reference network at a mean vessel radius of 20 microns (no vessel dropout), against which relative network resistance and functional derecruitment at larger mean radii were compared; and comparison of shear targets of 10 vs 20 Pa.

No comparative statistics (no OR/HR/CI/p-values) were reported — this is a deterministic simulation.

pico_ko.outcome: 전체 shear target을 유지하는 데 필요한 최소 심박출량은 평균 혈관 반지름에 따라 거의 선형으로 증가함(총 유량 요구는 혈관 반지름 세제곱의 합에 비례). shear target 20 Pa, SD 1.5 microns에서 평균 반지름 25 microns일 때 약 5 L/min, 30 microns일 때 약 8.5 L/min. 비교 통계(OR/HR/CI/p-value)는 보고되지 않았으며, 결정론적(deterministic) 시뮬레이션임.

keyFindings: In a one-million-vessel simulation, total cardiac output required to maintain a fixed shear target rises in proportion to the sum of vessel radii cubed, so even modest global vasodilation sharply increases required flow — at shear 20 Pa/SD 1.5 microns, from ~5 L/min (mean radius 25 microns) to ~8.5 L/min (30 microns).

When cardiac output cannot meet demand, low-shear vessels functionally derecruit: increasing mean radius from 25 to 30 microns at a fixed 5000 mL/min caused almost 50% vessel dropout with network resistance falling to 33% of baseline, reproducing the reduced functional capillary density and hyperdynamic, low-resistance circulation of severe sepsis.

entries.fullText:
### Background
Sepsis is the most common cause of circulatory failure treated in the intensive care unit. Despite this, a consistent understanding of the pathophysiology has been elusive. At first, it was thought sepsis was a standard low output shock state. Widespread use of the pulmonary artery catheter in the 60s and 70s first revealed the more common hyperdynamic, low resistance situation [, ]. At the same time animal and ex-vivo studies showed widespread arteriolar vasodilation and hypo-responsiveness of micro-vessels to catecholamines and other vasoconstricting agents [, ]. The next several decades of therapeutic theory focussed on enhancing oxygen delivery through targeting ScvO2 and supranormal cardiac index, which ultimately proved fruitless [, ].
Since the turn of the millennium access to new imaging techniques like handheld videomicroscopy has revealed the importance of microcirculatory dysfunction. Capillary perfusion is heterogeneous, with reduced functional capillary density and perfused vessel density, and increased stop flow capillaries [, ]. These changes may persist despite normalisation of macrocirculatory variables such as blood pressure and cardiac output and predict mortality; the so-called haemodynamic incoherence []. A satisfactory explanation for this disconnect has not emerged. Why a global vasodilatory process should lead to such a patchy locally heterogeneous problem is unclear. While it is possible that the two phenomena are completely separate, purely vasodilatory conditions such as calcium channel blocker overdose also result in the same microvascular impairment despite high global flow [].
Shear stress is the force exerted by flowing blood on the endothelium, in the direction parallel to the vessel wall. Shear signalling in normal endothelium involves feedback that maintains shear stress within vessels within a given range. Shear is increased by flow and by a reduction in vessel size. When shear is excessive, the signalling pathway will result in local release of nitric oxide (NO), which dilates the vessel and reduces the shear stress back to an acceptable range. Low shear produces endothelin, which constricts the vessel. Sensing of shear occurs via mechanotransduction proteins in the endothelial glycocalyx and gap junctions. Shear regulation is prominent within the microcirculation [, ].
Shear signalling is known to be abnormal in septic shock. Excessive expression of inducible NO synthase (iNOS) results in elevated levels of NO []. At the same time endothelial damage disrupts mechanotransducers and inflammatory cytokines inhibit signalling pathways, resulting in impairment of shear sensing [, ]. These observations have been linked generally to the development of microvascular dysfunction, but not through a mechanistic hypothesis.
The aim of this paper is to put forward a hypothesis that explains these disparate findings, and model how the microcirculation is constrained by three main factors; total cardiac output, vasomotor state, and shear regulation. Predictable behaviour of the microcirculation naturally emerges from changes in these variables.

### Methods
A microcirculatory network consisting of one million parallel arterioles was simulated in Python using JupyterLab (v 4.3.4). The distribution of vessel radii was simulated with a truncated normal distribution (with lower and upper physiological bounds of 0 and 100 microns). The mean vessel radius was varied between 20 and 30 microns, and the standard deviation between 1.5 and 3 microns, consistent with the typical size of arterioles in the microcirculation []. Typical numbers of parallel arterioles in the human body are unknown exactly, but probably closer to 100 million (based on roughly 10 capillaries and each arteriole feeding a bed of 100 capillaries [, ]), however the simulation was scaled for ease of computation, as explained further below.

### Model parameters
Shear stress in the arterioles, where the Womersley number < < 1, is given by the laminar shear equation;
is shear stress in Pa, µ is dynamic viscosity in Pa·s, Q is mean flow in m·s, and r is radius in metres.
A typical value of 0.003 Pa·s was used for dynamic viscosity. Although blood is shear thinning and changes its viscosity depending on velocity and vessel size, these effects were considered overall negligible on the outcome of the model.
The typical value of shear stress in arterioles is unknown, but has been observed to be 1–7 Pa in larger arteries and ten times less in capillaries [, ]. However given that the model is roughly scaled down in total vessel number by a factor of 100, 10–20 Pa was used as the model parameter. This is a scaled parameter that assumes that the typical value of shear in arterioles is closer to that in capillaries (multiplied by 100) and that produces realistic cardiac outputs in the model output. This is assumed to be a target value. In real vascular networks, the shear target will vary slightly from vessel to vessel, and regulation will not be exact, however for the purposes of the model I assume a single value across the network. Equation 1 can then be re-arranged as follows;
This is the flow that each vessel will demand to satisfy its apparent shear target (). I refer to an apparent shear target as the endothelium’s sensing of shear may not be perfectly calibrated to the actual level of shear. If sensing is perfect then actual shear is equal to apparent shear. However if shear sensing is inhibited, the vessel will constrict even though actual shear may be adequate. Thus, the apparent shear target will be raised.
For a given distribution of vessel sizes within the network, the total cardiac output required to satisfy the shear target in all vessels is simply the sum of all individual values of Q.

### Model output
The model calculated Q for each vessel in the one million vessel network based on the set apparent shear target and the distribution of vessel sizes. It displayed the minimum cardiac output required to satisfy the global shear target plotted against the mean vessel radius, with sliders to adjust the standard deviation of vessel radii between 1.5 and 3 microns, and apparent shear target between 10 and 20 Pa. Rightward movement along the x-axis therefore represents attempted uniform vasodilatation. Plots of vessel size distribution at each standard deviation, for a mean of 25 microns, were also produced. Units were converted from the SI units used in the shear equation to mL·min for flow and microns for vessel radius.
If cardiac output is not able to meet the minimal requirement, some vessels will experience shear below their target and constrict in response. Hypothetically, vessel de-recruitment should occur and vessels will be lost from the network, decreasing the overall flow requirement until an equilibrium is achieved again, at a reduced functional capillary density. Such an idealised response was implemented in this model in order to study network flow shunting behaviour, accepting that such regulation is not perfect in real biological systems. The model therefore also simulated a fixed cardiac output of 5000 mL·min, with standard deviation 1.5 microns and shear target 20 Pa. As mean vessel radius was increased from 20 to 30 microns, it iteratively removed the smallest vessels from the distribution, updating the distribution until total shear requirements were satisfied. It displayed a plot of vessel distribution, the total number of remaining vessels out of the initial million, and the relative resistance of the network compared to a baseline mean radius of 20 microns (where there was no vessel dropout). The relative resistance is R calculated from Eq.  in the updated distribution divided by that in the distribution at a mean of 20 microns.

### Results
Over the studied vessel range the minimum cardiac output required to maintain the global shear target increased almost linearly with the mean vessel radius. The required cardiac output, at a shear target of 20 Pa and standard deviation of 1.5 microns, increased from approximately 5 L per minute at a mean radius of 25 microns, to approximately 8.5 L per minute at a radius of 30 microns (Fig. ). Illustrative distribution of vessel sizes at a mean of 25 microns is shown in Fig. .

An increase in the standard deviation of vessel size from 1.5 microns to 3 microns resulted in a very small shift of the curve upwards, as illustrated in Fig. . A decrease in target shear from 20 to 10 Pa resulted in a significant drop in cardiac output requirement, halving it for the same mean vessel radius and for a given standard deviation of vessel radii (Fig. ).
For the fixed cardiac output analysis, at a mean of 25 microns, all of the initial one million vessels remained recruited and resistance was 41% of that at a mean of 20 microns. Increasing the mean to 30 microns resulted in almost 50% vessel dropout (similar to the reduction of functional capillary density observed in severe sepsis) and a resistance 33% of baseline. The drop in resistance would have been 20% of baseline without any vessel dropout. The latter distribution is shown in Fig. .

### Discussion
I have illustrated a hypothetical model of the microcirculation that is constrained by three elements; cardiac output, shear regulation, and vasomotor state. The model produces the characteristic microcirculatory changes seen in vasodilatory shock. When the microcirculation dilates (increases the sum of its radii cubed), cardiac output must increase in proportion to the sum of radii cubed in order to preserve the same level of shear. If this cannot be achieved, then vasoconstrictive responses triggered by low shear will close off branches of the microcirculation, reducing functional capillary density. entries.title: Advanced haemodynamic monitoring in cardiogenic shock.
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 심장성 쇼크(cardiogenic shock) 환자에서 어떤 고급 혈역학 모니터링 방법(예: advanced echocardiography, pulmonary artery catheterization)이 쇼크 표현형을 규명하고 예후 정보를 제공하며 개별화된 치료를 안내하는 데 가장 유용한가?

pico.population: [Country NR] Patients with cardiogenic shock, including phenotypes of isolated right ventricular failure, biventricular dysfunction, and mixed cardiogenic shock (narrative review; no sample size, age, or date range reported).
pico.intervention: Advanced/multimodal haemodynamic monitoring — including pulmonary artery catheterization and advanced echocardiography, and individual variables such as the velocity time integral (VTI) at the left ventricular outflow tract, pulmonary artery pulsatility index (PAPi), and cardiac power output (CPO) — used for phenotypic profiling, prognostication, risk classification, and guiding management.
pico.outcome: No primary outcome with reported statistics; as a narrative review the paper reports qualitatively that multimodal monitoring enables identification of cardiogenic shock phenotypes and that VTI at the LVOT, PAPi, and CPO are 'prognostically significant' (no numeric effect sizes, CIs, or p-values provided).

pico_ko.population: [국가 미기재] 심장성 쇼크(cardiogenic shock) 환자로, isolated right ventricular failure, biventricular dysfunction, mixed cardiogenic shock 표현형을 포함(narrative review로 표본 크기·연령·기간은 보고되지 않음).
pico_ko.intervention: 고급/다중모드 혈역학 모니터링 — pulmonary artery catheterization 및 advanced echocardiography, 그리고 left ventricular outflow tract에서의 velocity time integral(VTI), pulmonary artery pulsatility index(PAPi), cardiac power output(CPO) 같은 개별 변수를 포함 — 를 표현형 프로파일링, 예후 예측, 위험 분류, 치료 안내에 사용.
pico_ko.outcome: 통계치가 보고된 primary outcome은 없음; narrative review로서 다중모드 모니터링이 심장성 쇼크 표현형 식별을 가능하게 하고 VTI at LVOT, PAPi, CPO가 '예후적으로 유의하다(prognostically significant)'고 정성적으로 기술(수치적 effect size·CI·p-value 미제시).

keyFindings: Haemodynamic monitoring in cardiogenic shock is multimodal, providing complementary information that enables identification of distinct phenotypes — isolated right ventricular failure, biventricular dysfunction, and mixed cardiogenic shock — each associated with poor outcomes and requiring different management strategies.

Individual haemodynamic variables — velocity time integral (VTI) at the left ventricular outflow tract, pulmonary artery pulsatility index (PAPi), and cardiac power output (CPO) — are described as prognostically significant (no numeric effect sizes reported in the abstract).

Recent studies support the early use of pulmonary artery catheters to guide management; combined pulmonary artery catheterization plus advanced echocardiography helps tailor therapy, with ongoing trials expected to inform timing and use.
keyFindings_ko: 심장성 쇼크의 혈역학 모니터링은 다중모드(multimodal)로, 상호보완적 정보를 제공해 서로 다른 표현형 — isolated right ventricular failure, biventricular dysfunction, mixed cardiogenic shock — 을 식별하게 하며, 각각 예후가 나쁘고 서로 다른 관리 전략을 요한다.
개별 혈역학 변수 — left ventricular outflow tract에서의 velocity time integral(VTI), pulmonary artery pulsatility index(PAPi), cardiac power output(CPO) — 이 예후적으로 유의하다고 기술됨(초록에는 수치적 effect size 미보고).
최신 연구들은 관리 지침을 위한 pulmonary artery catheter의 조기 사용을 지지하며, pulmonary artery catheterization과 advanced echocardiography의 병용이 치료 개별화에 도움이 되고, 진행 중인 임상시험이 시점과 사용법을 밝힐 것으로 기대됨.

entries.title: Feeding under support in critical care illness: metabolic and nutritional management during extracorporeal membrane oxygenation and continuous renal replacement therapy.
entries.title_ko: 생명유지 중재 하의 중환자 영양: 체외막산소공급(ECMO)과 지속적신대체요법(CRRT) 중의 대사 및 영양 관리
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: ECMO, 기계순환보조(MCS), 그리고/또는 CRRT로 지지받는 중환자에서, 이 집단에 특화된 가이드라인이 없는 상황에서 대사·영양 평가와 공급(칼로리-단백 목표 설정, 미량영양소 보충, 모니터링)을 어떻게 관리해야 하는가?

pico.population: [Country NR] Critically ill patients receiving life-support therapies like extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO), mechanical circulatory systems (MCS), and continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT), for whom no specific nutrition guidelines exist; this is a mini-review and no sample size (N), age, or date range is reported.
pico.intervention: Pragmatic nutritional assessment and delivery strategies — calorie-protein targeting, micronutrient repletion, and monitoring — using a 'start low, advance carefully' approach within a multidisciplinary framework.
pico.outcome: Narrative synthesis only; no primary clinical outcome, effect size, or statistic is reported. The review characterizes pathophysiological/metabolic alterations (dramatic nutrient fluxes with CRRT, a possible hypermetabolic state exacerbated by ECMO, fluid overload constraints, drug-nutrient interactions) and proposes practical recommendations.

pico_ko.population: [국가 미기재] 체외막산소공급(ECMO), 기계순환보조(MCS), 지속적신대체요법(CRRT) 같은 생명유지 중재를 받는 중환자로, 이 집단에 특화된 영양 가이드라인은 존재하지 않는다. 본 논문은 mini-review이며 표본 크기(N)·연령·기간은 보고되지 않았다.
pico_ko.intervention: 실용적 영양 평가 및 공급 전략 — 칼로리-단백 목표 설정, 미량영양소 보충, 모니터링 — 을 다학제 틀 안에서 '낮게 시작해 천천히 증량(start low, advance carefully)'하는 접근으로 적용.
pico_ko.outcome: 서술적 종합(narrative synthesis)만 제시되며, 일차 임상 결과·효과크기·통계치는 보고되지 않음. CRRT로 인한 급격한 영양소 유출, ECMO로 악화될 수 있는 hypermetabolic state, 체액 과부하 제약, 약물-영양소 상호작용 등 병태생리·대사 변화를 기술하고 실용적 권고를 제안함.

keyFindings: No specific nutrition guidelines exist for critically ill patients on ECMO, MCS, or CRRT; current recommendations are largely based on expert opinion and observational data.
Key metabolic challenges are dramatic nutrient fluxes with CRRT, a possible hypermetabolic state exacerbated by ECMO, fluid overload constraints, and drug-nutrient interactions.
The authors propose a pragmatic 'start low, advance carefully' approach to calorie-protein targeting, micronutrient repletion, and monitoring within a multidisciplinary framework.
keyFindings_ko: ECMO, MCS, CRRT를 받는 중환자를 위한 특화된 영양 가이드라인은 존재하지 않으며, 현재 권고는 대부분 전문가 의견과 관찰 데이터에 근거한다.
주요 대사적 난제는 CRRT로 인한 급격한 영양소 유출, ECMO로 악화될 수 있는 hypermetabolic state, 체액 과부하 제약, 약물-영양소 상호작용이다.
저자들은 다학제 틀 안에서 칼로리-단백 목표 설정, 미량영양소 보충, 모니터링에 대해 실용적인 'start low, advance carefully' 접근을 제안한다.

entries.title: Consensus Recommendations for Clinical Pharmacist Integration into the Acute Stroke Care Team: Endorsed by the American College of Clinical Pharmacy, Neurocritical Care Society, Society for Academic Emergency Medicine, and Society of Critical Care Medicine, and affirmed by the American Academy of Neurology.
entries.title_ko: 급성 뇌졸중 진료팀에 임상약사를 통합하기 위한 합의 권고안: American College of Clinical Pharmacy, Neurocritical Care Society, Society for Academic Emergency Medicine, Society of Critical Care Medicine 승인 및 American Academy of Neurology 지지
entries.journal: Journal of the American College of Clinical Pharmacy : JACCP
entries.clinicalQuestion_ko: 응급실에 내원한 성인 acute ischemic stroke(AIS) 또는 hemorrhagic stroke 환자의 학제간 급성 뇌졸중 진료팀에 임상약사를 통합해야 하는가?

pico.population: [USA] Adult patients presenting to the emergency department (ED) with acute ischemic stroke (AIS) or hemorrhagic stroke. This consensus statement panel was composed of clinical pharmacists, emergency medicine physicians, and a vascular neurologist; two focused questions were addressed. (Exact panel size, number of included studies, and voting percentages not reported in the abstract.)
pico.intervention: Clinical pharmacist involvement/integration into the interdisciplinary acute stroke care team — specifically (1) clinical pharmacist involvement in AIS care for adult patients presenting to the ED and (2) clinical pharmacist involvement in hemorrhagic stroke care for adult patients presenting to the ED.
pico.outcome: Despite the very low level of evidence (GRADE approach used to assess certainty and strength), a consensus was reached endorsing both recommendations: clinical pharmacist involvement in AIS care and in hemorrhagic stroke care in the ED. No pooled effect estimates, confidence intervals, or p-values are reported (narrative consensus statement).

pico_ko.population: [미국] 응급실(ED)에 내원한 성인 acute ischemic stroke(AIS) 또는 hemorrhagic stroke 환자. 본 합의 성명 패널은 임상약사, 응급의학과 의사, 그리고 1명의 vascular neurologist로 구성되었으며 두 개의 핵심 질문을 다루었다. (정확한 패널 인원 수, 포함된 연구 수, 투표 비율은 초록에 미기재.)
pico_ko.intervention: 학제간 급성 뇌졸중 진료팀에 임상약사를 참여/통합 — 구체적으로 (1) 응급실에 내원한 성인 AIS 진료에 임상약사 참여, (2) 응급실에 내원한 성인 hemorrhagic stroke 진료에 임상약사 참여.
pico_ko.outcome: 근거 수준이 very low임에도 불구하고(certainty와 recommendation 강도 평가에 GRADE 방식 사용), 두 권고안 모두에 대해 합의가 도출되었다: 응급실에서 AIS 진료 및 hemorrhagic stroke 진료에 대한 임상약사 참여. 통합 효과 추정치, 신뢰구간, p-value는 보고되지 않았다(서술형 합의 성명).

keyFindings: A multidisciplinary panel (clinical pharmacists, emergency medicine physicians, and a vascular neurologist) using the GRADE approach reached consensus on two recommendations supporting clinical pharmacist involvement in ED care of adult AIS and hemorrhagic stroke patients.
Both recommendations were made despite a 'very low level of evidence,' underscoring that support rests on expert consensus rather than high-certainty comparative data.

The statement, endorsed by ACCP, NCS, SAEM, and SCCM and affirmed by AAN, frames optimizing the clinical pharmacist role as a critical factor for improving time-sensitive treatment and equity in stroke care delivery.
keyFindings_ko: 다학제 패널(임상약사, 응급의학과 의사, vascular neurologist 1명)이 GRADE 방식을 사용하여 성인 AIS 및 hemorrhagic stroke 환자의 ED 진료에 임상약사 참여를 지지하는 두 개의 권고안에 대해 합의를 도출했다.
두 권고안 모두 'very low level of evidence'에도 불구하고 도출되었으며, 이는 고확실성 비교 데이터가 아닌 전문가 합의에 기반함을 강조한다.
ACCP, NCS, SAEM, SCCM이 승인하고 AAN이 지지한 본 성명은 임상약사 역할 최적화를 시간에 민감한 치료와 뇌졸중 진료 형평성 개선의 핵심 요소로 규정한다.
